policy.title: RBI finalizes climate risk disclosure framework for scheduled commercial banks
policy.description: RBI released final guidelines requiring all scheduled commercial banks to conduct climate stress tests annually and report portfolio-level physical risk exposures by March 2027. Banks must integrate climate-adjusted PD/LGD into ICAAP submissions starting FY2027-28.

policy.title: SEBI extends BRSR Core to top 500 listed entities with mandatory assurance
policy.description: SEBI mandated BRSR Core reporting with limited assurance for top 500 companies by market cap from FY2026-27, expanding from the previous top 150. Value chain (Scope 3) disclosures become mandatory for top 250 entities.

policy.title: IRDAI releases sandbox framework for parametric climate insurance products
policy.description: IRDAI announced a regulatory sandbox allowing insurers and banks to pilot parametric products covering flood, drought, and cyclone perils with streamlined approval. Maximum coverage limits set at ₹50 crore per policy during sandbox phase.

policy.title: Basel Committee publishes Pillar 3 climate disclosure standards
policy.description: BCBS finalized Pillar 3 disclosure requirements for climate-related financial risks, mandating standardized templates for transition and physical risk exposures. Implementation deadline set for January 2028 for G-SIBs, with emerging market banks following by 2029.

policy.title: NGFS releases Phase V climate scenarios with enhanced physical risk pathways
policy.description: NGFS published updated scenarios incorporating chronic physical risks and tipping point analysis, with new sector-specific damage functions. India-specific downscaling now includes district-level agricultural productivity impacts through 2100.

policy.title: ISSB announces transition relief for emerging markets adopting IFRS S2
policy.description: ISSB approved a two-year transition period for emerging market jurisdictions adopting S2 climate standards, allowing phased Scope 3 reporting. India's adoption roadmap expected in Q2 2026 following MCA consultation.

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence partners with HDFC Bank for physical risk analytics
competitors.description: Jupiter announced a multi-year partnership with HDFC Bank to provide asset-level flood and cyclone risk scoring for the bank's corporate lending portfolio. This marks Jupiter's first major banking client in India.

competitors.title: GIST Advisory raises $12M Series B for natural capital analytics expansion
competitors.description: Mumbai-based GIST Advisory closed a $12M Series B led by Omidyar Network to expand its natural capital valuation platform targeting Indian banks and asset managers. Funds will support hiring 40 additional data scientists.

competitors.title: XDI launches India Physical Risk Database covering 5 million commercial assets
competitors.description: XDI released an India-specific physical risk database with granular flood, heat, and cyclone projections for 5 million geocoded commercial and industrial assets. The product targets Indian NBFCs and foreign banks with India exposure.

competitors.title: Moody's integrates physical risk scores into India corporate credit ratings
competitors.description: Moody's announced that climate physical risk assessments will be explicitly factored into credit opinions for 200+ rated Indian corporates starting Q2 2026. Methodology weights chronic risks including water stress and heat exposure.

competitors.title: Sprih partners with Yes Bank for BRSR compliance automation
competitors.description: Bengaluru-based Sprih signed an enterprise deal with Yes Bank to automate BRSR Core data collection across 150+ branches. The engagement includes Scope 3 emissions tracking for the bank's financed emissions.

competitors.title: Persefoni launches financed emissions module aligned with PCAF India guidance
competitors.description: Persefoni released a PCAF-aligned module specifically calibrated for Indian financial institutions, covering priority asset classes including commercial real estate and project finance. The product integrates with RBI's proposed reporting templates.

research.title: Climate Risk and Financial Stability in South Asia: A Banking Sector Assessment
research.description: Analysis of 85 South Asian banks finds physical climate risks could increase NPAs by 2.3-4.1 percentage points under a 2°C scenario by 2040. Indian PSU banks show highest vulnerability due to agricultural lending concentration.

research.title: Parametric Insurance for Climate Resilience: Lessons from Emerging Markets
research.description: Swiss Re finds parametric products reduced claims settlement time by 87% versus indemnity products in pilot programs across 12 emerging markets. Report highlights basis risk as the primary adoption barrier for agricultural borrowers.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Implications for Capital Flows
research.description: IFC study reveals that 68% of emerging market companies lack basic climate disclosures, contributing to a 40-60 bps cost of capital premium. India ranks highest among BRICS nations for disclosure coverage but trails developed markets significantly.

research.title: Physical Risk Transmission to Credit Risk: A Framework for Indian Banks
research.description: CRISIL proposes methodology linking district-level flood and drought indices to sectoral PD adjustments, estimating ₹1.2-1.8 lakh crore in climate-vulnerable exposures across Indian banking system. SME and MSME portfolios identified as most data-deficient.

research.title: Supervisory Expectations for Climate Stress Testing: Implementation Stocktake
research.description: FSB survey of 24 jurisdictions finds only 8 have fully operationalized climate stress testing requirements. Report recommends standardized physical risk scenario parameters and notes data availability as the primary implementation challenge.

research.title: Natural Catastrophe Protection Gap: India Market Analysis
research.description: Munich Re estimates India's climate protection gap at $48 billion annually, with only 6% of economic losses from floods and cyclones insured. Report identifies parametric solutions as critical to closing the gap for commercial and agricultural exposures.

ourRead.headline: RBI's final climate framework transforms physical risk modelling from advisory to compliance requirement — StepChange must move fast before global players entrench.
body: This month's signals converge on a single theme: India's climate risk infrastructure is moving from aspiration to mandate, and the window for StepChange to establish dominance is narrowing. RBI's final guidelines, IRDAI's parametric sandbox, and SEBI's BRSR expansion are creating simultaneous demand across all three of our strategic bets — but so is competition from well-capitalized global and local players.

The RBI framework (policy-1) is the headline event. By requiring climate-adjusted PD/LGD in ICAAP submissions from FY2027-28, the regulator has created non-negotiable demand for precisely what StepChange builds. The March 2027 stress test deadline means banks must begin procurement in the next 6-9 months. Critically, the CRISIL research (research-4) identifying ₹1.2-1.8 lakh crore in climate-vulnerable exposures provides the business case language our sales team needs — banks now have both regulatory pressure and quantified portfolio risk to justify investment.

The competitor signals demand immediate attention. Jupiter's HDFC partnership (comp-1) and XDI's 5-million-asset India database (comp-3) show global players are no longer treating India as a future market — they're here now with localized products. However, their pricing and customization limitations create opportunity: Moody's integration of physical risk into credit ratings (comp-4) actually helps us by validating the methodology, while their enterprise pricing keeps them out of the NBFC and mid-tier bank segment where StepChange should concentrate.

The Swiss Re parametric research (research-2) is actionable for our second strategic bet. The 87% claims settlement improvement is a compelling proof point, but the explicit call-out of basis risk as the adoption barrier tells us what to build: our parametric product design work must prioritize basis risk minimization through hyperlocal trigger calibration. IRDAI's sandbox (policy-3) with its ₹50 crore coverage cap suggests starting with mid-sized commercial exposures rather than large corporate books.

Watch: The NGFS Phase V scenarios (policy-5) with India district-level downscaling — this becomes the de facto reference for any bank stress test, so we need to ensure our models align. Action: Prioritize a commercial partnership announcement with one PSU bank in the next 90 days to counter Jupiter's HDFC win — credibility in the public sector banking space is what differentiates a domestic specialist from a foreign entrant.
